name: Claude Code Repo Sync
description: Git pull → process → commit → push cycle for agents operating on a shared repository. Ensures agents always work on fresh state and all changes are committed atomically with full traceability.

inputs.description: Working directory state before the agent run (after git pull).
Files to be written or modified during the agent's run.

outputs.description: Attributed commit with Co-Authored-By agent identity, pushed to a feature branch.
PR opened when output requires human review before merge into main.

permissions.justification: Agents must pull latest state before acting and push their outputs to a feature branch for human review.

constraints: git pull must be the first action — never read or modify repo state before pulling.
Never push directly to main, master, or any protected branch.
Every commit must include a Co-Authored-By attribution line identifying the agent and model.
Commit message must reference the originating mission brief ID or signal ID.
work/ directory writes are additive only — never overwrite existing signals, releases, or postmortems.
All governance artifacts (policy/, CODEOWNERS, AGENTS.md) are read-only for execution agents — never modify.

changelog.change: Initial version. Git sync cycle skill ensuring all Claude Code agents operate on fresh repo state and commit with full attribution.
